entities.name: Yolo-Auto
entities.description: Verified from the site: an OpenAI-compatible API service offering unlimited Qwen3.6-35B-A3B inference for a flat $6/month, with a 128K context window and no per-token metering, aimed at coding agents, RAG pipelines, and CI/CD bots.
entities.freshness_signal: © 2026 footer; promotional pricing '40% off active now' (reduced from $10 to $6/month)

entities.name: mcp-test-harness
entities.description: Verified from the repo: a pytest-style testing framework for MCP servers with MCP-specific assertions, functional/regression/performance test modes, multi-format reporting, parallel execution, and CI/CD (GitHub Actions) integration.
entities.freshness_signal: Release 2.0.0 on PyPI/GHCR, 690+ internal tests, 100% line coverage

entities.name: Routing24 Route Optimizer
entities.description: Verified from the repo: a Claude/Cowork Agent Skill that converts natural-language stop and vehicle lists into an optimized, shareable multi-stop route plan; route optimization runs client-side in the browser with no API key or sign-in required.

entities.name: ContextNest
entities.description: Verified from the article: a version-controlled, governed context management system operating as a native MCP server, using local-first/self-hosted markdown vaults versioned with Git and SHA-256 hash chains, with manual steward approval before knowledge enters agent context and deterministic 'ctx forget' removal.

entities.name: Mem0
entities.description: Verified from the article: a semantic graph memory framework (mem0.ai) that extracts and maintains user-specific preferences and habits across chat streams via autonomous semantic auto-merges with no manual review.

entities.name: Zep
entities.description: Verified from the article: a conversational memory platform (getzep.com) that runs auto-summarization and message-indexing pipelines over session logs and chat histories to maintain session continuity.

entities.name: Rainslice
entities.description: Verified from the site: an AI platform automating end-to-end operations for home services businesses (cleaning, HVAC, plumbing, etc.), handling call/text answering, lead qualification, scheduling, dispatch routing, and quote generation 24/7.
entities.freshness_signal: © 2026 footer, active go-to-market phase with demo booking CTAs

entities.name: mcp-use
entities.description: Verified from the site: an open-source SDK (TypeScript and Python) for scaffolding, testing, and building MCP applications and servers, with one-command scaffolding and a Visual Inspector for testing without a live LLM.
entities.freshness_signal: 7M+ SDK downloads, 10k+ GitHub stars; no explicit version given

entities.name: Manufact Cloud
entities.description: Verified from the site: a hosting platform for deploying mcp-use applications with automatic GitHub-push deployment (no YAML/Docker config), multi-platform support (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, coding agents), and app-store/connector publishing.
entities.freshness_signal: deployment under 60 seconds from code push to live; no explicit version given

entities.name: PromptQL Tag
entities.description: Verified from the site: a company-wide AI agent for Slack/Microsoft Teams that answers questions, analyzes data, and generates content while respecting each user's individual permissions, maintains shared team knowledge as a wiki, and connects to databases/APIs.
entities.freshness_signal: new research collaboration with UC Berkeley mentioned; © 2026 footer

entities.name: MCP SDK betas (2026-07-28 spec RC)
entities.description: Verified from the post: beta releases of the four Tier 1 MCP SDKs (Python v2, TypeScript v2, Go v1.7.0-pre.1, C# v2.0.0-preview.1) implementing the 2026-07-28 spec release candidate, which makes the protocol stateless (removing the initialize handshake and protocol-level session).

entities.name: Parsewise
entities.description: Verified from the site: a RESTful API platform for multi-document processing and structured data extraction, offering cross-document entity linking and contradiction detection across 10,000+ pages per run, targeted at insurance, asset management, and lending firms.
entities.maintainer_or_vendor: unknown (built with Claude; no parent company named on page)

entities.name: Supafax
entities.description: Verified from the site: an AI email assistant for Gmail/Outlook that drafts replies in the user's voice, auto-schedules meetings, auto-labels emails, and supports custom automation via natural-language rules, treating email as a task-management file system.
entities.freshness_signal: © 2026 footer; $35/mo Professional plan with 14-day free trial

entities.name: Trajeckt
entities.description: Verified from the repo: a runtime enforcement gateway that blocks multi-step data-exfiltration tool-call chains even when each call is individually authorized, using sealed cryptographic commitments and taint-propagation tracking, fail-closed by default, implementing the MCP Streamable HTTP transport.

entities.name: Evaluation Context Protocol (ECP)
entities.description: Verified from the site: a vendor-neutral, JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol standardizing how evaluators inspect agent tool usage and audit context (not just final answers) across frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, PydanticAI) and CI systems, positioned as an 'evaluation contract layer' analogous to MCP.
entities.maintainer_or_vendor: unknown (open source, creator not named on page)

entities.name: Hail.so
entities.description: Verified from the site: an MCP server, CLI, and REST API unifying voice calls, SMS/MMS, and email for AI agents through a single integration point, with real-time call transcripts, TCPA compliance, and STIR/SHAKEN authentication.
entities.freshness_signal: site shows 'AVAILABLE NOW'; self-hostable under AGPL or managed cloud, 2026

entities.name: Agent Swarm
entities.description: Verified from the site: an open-source operating system for coordinating AI agent work, where a lead agent breaks goals into tasks routed to specialized workers (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) in isolated Docker containers, with shared memory persisting across sessions; positioned as an open-source alternative to CrewAI, Claude Tag, and Devin.
entities.freshness_signal: 50,000 tasks executed in the last 30 days, 10+ daily installs; no explicit version number
